title: Sew up a torn seam
safetyTips: Keep fingers clear of the needle point.
Work in good light and take breaks to avoid eye strain.

steps.title: Turn the item inside out
steps.detail: This hides the stitches on the finished side.

steps.title: Pin the seam
steps.detail: Line up both edges and pin so the fabric stays put.

steps.title: Thread and knot
steps.detail: Thread the needle, double the thread, and tie a knot at the end.

steps.title: Sew small backstitches
steps.detail: Sew along the old seam line with small, even backstitches for strength.

steps.title: Knot and trim
steps.detail: Tie off the thread at the end and trim the extra.

title: Free a stuck zipper
safetyTips: Pull gently; forcing a zipper can tear the fabric.

steps.title: Find what's stuck
steps.detail: Check for fabric caught in the slider or bent teeth.

steps.title: Free caught fabric
steps.detail: Gently pull the fabric out while easing the slider back.

steps.title: Lubricate the teeth
steps.detail: Rub a graphite pencil or bar soap along the teeth.

steps.title: Work the slider
steps.detail: Slide up and down slowly until it moves freely.

title: Troubleshoot a lamp that won't turn on
safetyTips: Always unplug the lamp before touching the socket.
If you see damaged wiring, scorch marks, or a burning smell, stop and get professional help.
Do not attempt internal rewiring yourself.

steps.title: Unplug the lamp
steps.detail: Never work on a lamp while it is plugged in.

steps.title: Swap the bulb
steps.detail: Try a bulb you know works to rule out a dead bulb.

steps.title: Test the outlet
steps.detail: Plug the lamp into an outlet you know is working.

steps.title: Check the switch
steps.detail: See if the switch clicks and feels normal. If not, replace the whole lamp or ask a pro to service it.

title: Tighten wobbly furniture
safetyTips: Ask for a second set of hands when flipping heavy furniture.

steps.title: Find the loose joint
steps.detail: Rock the furniture gently to see which joint moves.

steps.title: Tighten hardware
steps.detail: Snug up any screws or bolts you find.

steps.title: Re-glue if needed
steps.detail: For loose wooden dowels, add a little wood glue and press together.

steps.title: Clamp and dry
steps.detail: Clamp or weigh the joint and let glue dry fully before use.

title: Patch a flat bike tube
tools: Bucket of water (to find the hole)
safetyTips: Let air out fully before removing the tire to avoid a sudden pop.

steps.title: Remove the wheel and tube
steps.detail: Use tire levers to pry one side of the tire off the rim.

steps.title: Find the hole
steps.detail: Inflate the tube and listen, or dip it in water to spot bubbles.

steps.title: Apply the patch
steps.detail: Rough the area, apply glue, and press the patch firmly.

steps.title: Reinstall and inflate
steps.detail: Put the tube and tire back, then inflate to the pressure on the tire wall.

title: Adjust bike brake pads (rim brakes)
safetyTips: Test the brakes at walking speed before any real ride.
If the brakes are hydraulic, or you are unsure, take the bike to a shop.
Never ride a bike whose brakes do not reliably stop it.

steps.title: Check pad wear
steps.detail: Replace pads if the grooves are worn smooth.

steps.title: Align the pads
steps.detail: Loosen the pad bolt and set the pad to hit the rim, not the tire.

steps.title: Set pad spacing
steps.detail: Adjust the cable so pads sit close but don't rub when released.

steps.title: Test carefully
steps.detail: Walk the bike and squeeze the levers to confirm it stops before riding.

title: Reinforce a frayed low-voltage cable
safetyTips: Only do this for low-voltage cables like USB or headphone cords.
If you see exposed copper on a wall-power cable, recycle it and replace it.
Never patch a frayed high-voltage or mains cable.

steps.title: Unplug the cable
steps.detail: Disconnect from both the device and power.

steps.title: Check the damage
steps.detail: If the inner wires are cut, replace the cable instead of patching.

steps.title: Add strain relief
steps.detail: Slide heat-shrink or wrap tape over the fray to support the bend point.

steps.title: Test gently
steps.detail: Reconnect and confirm it charges without wiggling.

title: Replace a dripping faucet washer
safetyTips: Turn off the water supply under the sink before starting.
If pipes are corroded or you can't stop the water, call a plumber.

steps.title: Shut off the water
steps.detail: Close the valves under the sink and open the faucet to drain it.

steps.title: Open the handle
steps.detail: Remove the handle screw and lift off the handle.

steps.title: Replace the washer
steps.detail: Swap the worn washer or cartridge for a matching new one.

steps.title: Reassemble and test
steps.detail: Put it back together, turn the water on slowly, and check for drips.
